blockers.where: P-22 / SPEC 4.A DEGENERACY GUARD and D-22 ('cohort with D_e inside patient-cluster bootstrap noise = no selection signal, neither gate pass nor fail'), interacting with D-20 and SPEC 4.F (IUT over 'ALL included cohorts')
blockers.issue: The degeneracy/vacuous determination is an OUTCOME-DEPENDENT cohort-inclusion decision. D_e = L^H(a#_e) - L^H(a*H_e) is the span of HELD-VIEW model losses, so declaring a cohort 'vacuous' requires reading held-view model outcomes across all 648 candidates. In an intersection-union test where every INCLUDED cohort must clear tau_G, dropping a vacuous cohort ('neither pass nor fail') SHRINKS the conjunction and makes the all-cohorts gate EASIER to pass. Worst case it drops Georgia — the SOLE cross-lineage cohort per Sec 1.6 — converting a within-Zheng-lineage-only result into a headline 'ACTIVE CONTROL DEMONSTRATED'. This directly contradicts D-20's promise that cohort inclusion is 'decided with zero model info', creating a second, outcome-driven exclusion path the freeze does not acknowledge.
blockers.fix: Pre-register the guard conservatively so it can NEVER ease the gate: a cohort found vacuous at analysis makes the all-cohorts replication UN-evaluable, so the program verdict is capped at 'WITH CAVEAT'/'INCONCLUSIVE' and can never be 'DEMONSTRATED'; a vacuous cohort counts as non-pass for the IUT, never as a silent drop. Explicitly reconcile D-20 (metadata-only inclusion) with D-22 (outcome-based vacuity) and state that vacuity may only DOWNGRADE, never UPGRADE, the verdict. Separately note that D_e/s_e used as gate denominators are comparison-invariant (same across policies) so standardizing G by D_e does not itself bias P0-vs-adaptive.

blockers.where: SPEC 4.D ('K = 10 policy seeds x M = 5 acquisition/held splits (frozen)') and Sec 4.5 ('re-evaluates the K x M runs') vs Sec 5.6 / P-20 (single deterministic split via SHA-256('C87_TARGET_SPLIT_V1'|cohort|patient_id), lower floor(n/2) -> ACQUISITION) and Sec 5.8 ('Held-evaluation view sealed', singular)
blockers.issue: Internal contradiction left uncaught by the reconciliation record. Sec 4 requires M=5 acquisition/held splits as a resample layer, but Sec 5.6 pins exactly ONE deterministic partition with no split index, and Sec 4.1/5.8 speak of a single sealed held view. Running 5 splits needs 5 partition rules that are NOT frozen (no per-split salt), 5 held views that must each be sealed and pass the min-support gate, and split-dependent standardizers a*H_e, D_e, T_e, s_e — none specified. This is an UNPINNED degree of freedom inside a document whose premise is that every DoF is PINNED or DEFERRED-by-rule, and an unpinned split-generation rule is resolvable with hindsight (post-hoc split choice is a prospective-integrity hole).
blockers.fix: Pick one and freeze it: either (a) M=1 (drop the M=5 layer, standardizers defined on the single Sec 5.6 held view), or (b) freeze M=5 by parameterizing Sec 5.6 with a per-split salt ('C87_TARGET_SPLIT_V1_split{m}', m=0..4), declare all 5 held views sealed until selection freeze, require each to pass the min-support gate (C87-E on failure), and state whether a*H_e/D_e/T_e are per-split then averaged or defined on a designated reference split. Add this to the reconciliation record.

blockers.where: P-25 / SPEC 4.D thresholds tau_G = 0.10 and tau_T = 0.05 (also ID row P-09 constants)
blockers.issue: tau_G and tau_T are asserted as frozen values with NO stated outcome-independent provenance. Because C86D (the prior EEG line) reports gain magnitudes (~0.006 < TAU) and a transport threshold, a red-teamer cannot rule out that these bars were calibrated against a prior real RESULT. They are almost certainly fine (no ECG target result exists yet), but a pre-registration must document that they are substantive-effect-size choices, not tuned to any outcome.
blockers.fix: Add a one-line provenance note: state tau_G/tau_T are pre-declared minimum scientifically-meaningful standardized effect sizes (or prior program DESIGN constants), explicitly NOT derived from any ECG target-model result, and cite their basis (label-cost/effect-size reasoning), mirroring how the budget ladder's provenance is documented in Sec 4.3.

blockers.where: RULE C87-RATE R2 (Sec 5.2) and QC Rule Q (Sec 5.3 / D-03): 'On a LABEL-FREE waveform sample, measure per-lead spectral-energy fraction / QRS morphology ...' and flat-lead/duration QC
blockers.issue: The r* choice (which fixes the frozen interface) and the QC exclusion floor are resolved by signal-integrity audits that may read TARGET-cohort waveforms. These are label/outcome-free (the doc says so), so they do not violate the letter of the freeze, but they make the frozen INTERFACE depend on target-cohort signal properties, which slightly undercuts the 'untouched target' framing and is an avoidable target-data dependence.
blockers.fix: Pin the R2 spectral/QRS audit and the flat-lead/duration QC calibration to SOURCE-cohort (or a public reference) waveforms only, or explicitly state that target waveforms may be read for signal-integrity but that the resulting r*/QC values are shown to be invariant to whether source or target waveforms are used (cohorts are all native 500 Hz, so this should hold trivially).

blockers.where: Scope banner and P-03 / Sec 1.5 wording 'UNTOUCHED target cohorts'
blockers.issue: The three targets are in fact metadata-inspected before freeze: their per-class SNOMED label counts are read for task selection (Sec 2) and their waveforms may be read for the r*/QC audits (Sec 5.2-5.3). 'UNTOUCHED' is precise only with respect to MODEL outcomes. The scope banner does permit metadata, but the strong word 'UNTOUCHED' overstates the isolation.
blockers.fix: Qualify to 'untouched with respect to any model OUTCOME (label-count metadata and waveform signal-integrity are inspected pre-freeze; held-view outcomes are sealed)'. This is a transparency edit, not a design change.

redteam.notes:
Red-team scope: outcome leakage + prospective integrity + independence-claim honesty. VERDICT NEEDS_REVISION because one BLOCKER and one MAJOR survive; both are fixable with pre-registration wording, no re-scoping needed.

WHAT IS CLEAN (verified): (1) Independence caveat (Sec 1.6) is HONEST and web-confirmed — Chapman 10,247 + Ningbo 34,905 = 45,152 = the combined Zheng/Chapman-University `ecg-arrhythmia` database, one 10-s ECG/patient, shared SNOMED/adjudication pipeline; the doc correctly refuses 'three independent cohorts' and feeds this to Cell-4 conditional-nontransportability. (2) Task-selection (Sec 2) is genuinely metadata-only and ACCURATE — every per-cohort SNOMED count in Sec 2.2 matches the public `evaluation-2021/dx_mapping_scored.csv` exactly, including Ningbo AF=0 (T1 inadmissibility) and SB counts (T3); the selection algorithm reads only counts, no model quantity. (3) Cohort sizes match Challenge-2021 v1.0.3 exactly (total 88,253). (4) Citations verified: MODEL SELECTOR = Okanovic AISTATS 2025 / arXiv 2410.13609; CODA = Kay 'Consensus-Driven Active Model Selection' / arXiv 2507.23771; LURE weight formula (P-13) matches Farquhar/Kossen. (5) Zoo is source-trained only; split is deterministic hash; synthetic controls (Sec 6) use frozen a-priori constants + real held-n metadata only; method primary/secondary status is decided by a synthetic toy-oracle audit, never a real ECG outcome. No already-contaminated frozen VALUE was found.

CORE DEFECT (BLOCKER): the only genuine outcome-dependent gate mechanism is the degeneracy/vacuity guard (D-22 / SPEC 4.A), which reads held-view model losses (D_e) to drop cohorts from the all-cohorts IUT and can thereby ease the gate or silently remove Georgia (the sole cross-lineage cohort). It contradicts D-20's 'zero model info' inclusion promise. Fix is a conservative-resolution clause (vacuity may only downgrade, never upgrade, the verdict).

SECONDARY (MAJOR): M=5 acquisition/held splits (Sec 4) contradicts the single deterministic split (Sec 5.6) — an unpinned split-generation DoF (no per-split salt, no per-split sealing) in a doc claiming full DoF-lock; resolvable with hindsight, so a prospective-integrity gap.

Three MINORs (threshold provenance, target-waveform dependence of r*/QC, 'UNTOUCHED' phrasing) are hardening/transparency edits. Once the BLOCKER and MAJOR wording fixes land, this section is freeze-ready.

blockers.where: R-1 / D-24; §4.1 SPEC 4.A ("PRIMARY = mean binary NLL over the loss label axis (S multi-label, OR single §2 task — D-24)"); §2.0 editor note; Appendix R-1
blockers.issue: The PRIMARY estimand's loss functional is left unpinned. Whether the selection loss is (a) binary NLL on the single §2 task or (b) mean binary NLL over the multi-label scored set S is explicitly UNRECONCILED and deferred to 'a single owner before C87E.' The two options define different a*H_e, T_e, R, G and therefore different gate outcomes — the entire object of the freeze. D-24 is a promise-to-decide, not a resolution rule, so it violates the doc's own standard ('resolved by an explicit NO-OUTCOME rule, stated as a rule, not a value'). A pre-registration whose primary loss is a post-freeze choice is not frozen on its primary endpoint.
blockers.fix: Pin option (a) single §2 binary task NOW in the freeze (already the editor's recommendation; §2 is the dedicated task owner and the label-adaptive methods map most faithfully to a single task). Delete D-24 as a deferral, rewrite SPEC 4.A to a single fixed label axis, and downgrade the multi-label reading to a pre-declared secondary robustness variant if desired.

blockers.where: §4.5 SPEC 4.E (G-a/G-b/G-c branches) vs §1.3 / §5.6 / D-04; interacts with §4.6 gate ('every INCLUDED cohort') and §1.6
blockers.issue: The 'ALL cohorts' gate is only as strong as the INCLUDED set, and the sole cross-lineage cohort (Georgia) can be excluded. Per §1.6 only Georgia-vs-{Chapman,Ningbo} is cross-lineage; Chapman and Ningbo are one Zheng lineage. But SPEC 4.E's decision tree routes Georgia to branch G-c, NOT G-b: G-b requires 'source documented one-record-per-patient,' which Georgia is NOT (Emory only states overlap is 'vanishingly small'); Georgia has no ID field and is not a documented singleton, so it satisfies exactly G-c's precondition. G-c(ii) permits 'REPORTED-BUT-EXCLUDED from the formal gate' on a 'material unresolved grouping' threshold that is never numerically pinned. There is NO rule capping the program verdict when Georgia is excluded, so 'ACTIVE CONTROL DEMONSTRATED' could rest on two same-lineage (cross-hospital) cohorts — precisely the confound §1.6 warns against. Other sections (§1.3, §5.6, D-04) treat Georgia as 'record=patient, proceed,' contradicting SPEC 4.E's exclusion-capable routing.
blockers.fix: Pin Georgia explicitly as record=patient / PROCEED-with-conservatism-caveat and NOT eligible for G-c(ii) exclusion. Numerically pin the G-c near-duplicate 'material grouping' threshold as a rule. Add a hard verdict rule: an unqualified DEMONSTRATED requires the sole cross-lineage cohort (Georgia) to be INCLUDED and passing; any exclusion of Georgia caps the maximum verdict at 'WITH CAVEAT / within-lineage only,' and state a minimum included-cohort count.

blockers.where: §4.1 SPEC 4.A DEGENERACY GUARD; D-22; §4.8 item 6
blockers.issue: The degeneracy-span guard is OUTCOME-DEPENDENT and vaguely thresholded — the one exclusion lever that reads model results. D_e = L^H(a#) - L^H(a*H) is a function of held-view MODEL losses; a cohort with D_e 'inside patient-cluster bootstrap noise' is declared VACUOUS ('neither gate pass nor fail'). The threshold is not a pinned statistic, and the disposition drops the cohort. A researcher who observes the active method failing in a cohort could contest that cohort's D_e as noise and remove it, again collapsing the 'all cohorts' gate — an outcome-conditioned researcher degree of freedom.
blockers.fix: Replace the prose guard with a pre-registered exact test (e.g., a pinned floor on LCB_95(D_e) with a stated numeric floor and pre-committed derivation), applied and logged before any G/R is read, and subject to the same verdict-cap rule as the cohort-exclusion fix (a vacuous Georgia cannot yield an unqualified positive).

blockers.where: §4.4 SPEC 4.D ('K=10 policy seeds x M=5 acquisition/held splits') vs P-20 and §5.6 (single deterministic SHA-256 floor(n/2) split)
blockers.issue: Internal contradiction plus an unpinned mechanism. P-20/§5.6 define exactly ONE deterministic acquisition/held partition (single salt 'C87_TARGET_SPLIT_V1', lower floor(n/2) -> acquisition). SPEC 4.D and §4.5 reference M=5 acquisition/held SPLITS and recompute a*H_e/D_e over K×M runs. No rule generates the 5 splits (no seed index or 5 salts are pinned), so the split family is a hidden free parameter; and 5 re-partitions conflict with the frozen invariants 'all of a patient's records in one view' and 'held-eval SEALED until selection freeze' (a patient would be acquisition in some splits and held in others).
blockers.fix: Reconcile to one option: either set M=1 (single deterministic split) and correct SPEC 4.D/§4.5, or pin an explicit deterministic 5-split generator (e.g., salt suffixed by split index 0..4) and restate the sealed-held and one-view invariants per split.

blockers.where: D-18 / §5.6 min-support gate (N_min, m_class) and D-03 / §5.3 QC (flat-lead count K, native-duration floor)
blockers.issue: Cohort/class inclusion in the gate depends on unpinned numeric thresholds. N_min, m_class, the flat-lead count K, and the duration floor are all 'fixed by the metadata/signal-integrity audit' with no value or formula given, yet a cohort/class failing them is C87-E'd out of the formal gate. These are outcome-free (metadata/waveform), but they remain hidden free parameters that shrink the 'all cohorts' set and feed the §2 support counts that drive task selection.
blockers.fix: Pin the numeric values (or a fully-specified metadata formula) for N_min, m_class, flat-lead K, and the duration floor in the freeze itself, so no post-freeze latitude over which cohorts/classes count toward the gate remains.

blockers.where: §5.2 RULE C87-RATE R2 (D-01)
blockers.issue: r* (which governs the entire 648-model zoo interface) is resolved by 'smallest r passing R1 & R2 & R3,' but R2's signal-integrity gates (spectral-energy fraction, QRS median-beat correlation, diagnostic-band energy) are each stated as '>= pre-registered thresholds' with no numeric values, and the tie-break defers partly to a 'resource envelope.' The audit is outcome-free so it cannot game the Measurement->Control conclusion, but the deciding thresholds are the actual unpinned knobs.
blockers.fix: Pin the numeric R2 thresholds (or bind each to a cited published ECG signal-quality/bandwidth standard) so r* is determined by a fully-specified rule rather than choosable audit tolerances.

blockers.where: §5.4 backbone (D-16)
blockers.issue: The shared 1D backbone's depth/width/kernel/pooling is deferred to an unnamed 'reference-impl rule, spec-hash frozen BEFORE training,' with no specific architecture or repository/commit named — unlike the method repos, which are explicitly pinned (D-13: jlko/active-testing, okanovic/model-selector, justinkay/coda). Outcome-free and architecture-controlled (low confound risk), but a pre-registration should name the reference it is committing to.
blockers.fix: Name the specific backbone reference implementation and commit and record its spec-hash in the freeze, mirroring the D-13 method-pinning discipline.

blockers.where: §4.6 SPEC 4.F (Holm family = registry\{P0}) and §3.3 CODA primary-conditional
blockers.issue: The Holm-Bonferroni family size depends on whether CODA passes the §3.4 audit (2 vs 3 methods x 7 budgets = 14 vs 21 hypotheses). The audit is outcome-free and pre-committed, so this is acceptable, but the freeze does not state that the family size is LOCKED at CONTROL_PASS time and immutable once any real p-value is computed.
blockers.fix: Add an explicit sentence: the Holm family membership and cardinality are fixed at the moment the §6 CONTROL_PASS token is signed and cannot change after any real target p-value is produced.

blockers.where: §3.1(d), P-12, §3.2 table (VMA citation)
blockers.issue: Author order is wrong and internally inconsistent. The paper is Hara, Matsuura, Honda et al., Machine Learning 2024 (DOI 10.1007/s10994-024-06603-1), but the doc cites 'VMA (Matsuura & Hara 2024)' in some places and 'Hara / Matsuura VMA' in others. Citation hygiene only; VMA is a real, verified secondary-tier method.
blockers.fix: Standardize to 'Hara, Matsuura, Honda et al. 2024' throughout.

redteam.notes:
Verdict NEEDS_REVISION: two BLOCKER-level researcher-degrees-of-freedom survive (unpinned primary selection loss; excludable sole cross-lineage cohort with no verdict cap), plus one outcome-DEPENDENT exclusion lever (degeneracy guard) and two other MAJORs.

What is SOLID (checked, no finding): (1) Empirical grounding — I web-verified the two label-adaptive methods (MODEL SELECTOR/Okanovic AISTATS 2025 arXiv 2410.13609; CODA/Kay ICCV 2025 Highlight arXiv 2507.23771), VMA (Hara/Matsuura/Honda, Machine Learning 2024), and the Chapman+Ningbo=45,152 one-ECG-per-patient identity (PhysioNet ecg-arrhythmia). The '>=1 prediction-driven + >=1 genuinely label-adaptive' requirement is met by real, correctly-classified methods. (2) Multiplicity/family handling is sound: IUT per (pi,B) with p^conj = max_e p_e needs no correction, Holm across the grid controls FWER, secondary methods (VMA/ASE) are excluded from the gate and cannot produce a DEMONSTRATED verdict — this prevents cherry-picking a winning (method,budget) cell. Recommend one reinforcing sentence stating secondary-method results can NEVER establish DEMONSTRATED/WITH CAVEAT. (3) The task-selection algorithm (§2.7) is well-frozen: metadata-count-driven, deterministic tie-break, signed manifest before scoring, non-binding expected outcome. (4) tau_G=0.10, tau_T=0.05, alpha=0.05, budget ladder (P-24), zoo topology (range(4,200,5)=40 checkpoints, 81/context, 648 total) are all correctly pinned and outcome-independent; the LURE weight formula (P-13) matches the published estimator. (5) The 'NEVER relax a threshold' rule (§4.6/§6.6) armors tau_G against subset-relaxation — the ONLY breach is via cohort exclusion, which is exactly what BLOCKER 2 / MAJOR 3 / MAJOR 5 target.

Already self-disclosed and not re-raised: 'XWED' estimator label unverified (ASE verified; secondary-only), PTB-XL 18,885 vs 18,869 patient count, |S| 26 vs 30 — all source-side/metadata and correctly flagged in the Appendix.

The common thread across the top findings: every remaining post-hoc lever operates through the INCLUDED-cohort set, which is the gate's only unsealed seam. Pin the primary loss, close the Georgia-exclusion pathway with a verdict cap, make the degeneracy guard an exact pre-registered test, and numerically pin the inclusion/QC/rate thresholds, and the freeze is defensible.

blockers.where: §6.1 generative model (loss_{v,a,p,r}); §6.2 POS crit-2; §6.3 NEG-B; §6.6 gating; and §3.4 TOY-ORACLE checks C/E
blockers.issue: The synthetic pipeline-validation gate (§6) AND the reference-fidelity freeze gate (§3.4) both define their synthetic worlds by a per-record LOSS tensor (§6.1 'loss_{v,a,p,r}'; §3.4 'K synthetic models with KNOWN per-record losses'). But the two genuinely-label-adaptive PRIMARY methods provably need more than a loss: MODEL SELECTOR (arXiv 2410.13609, source-verified) requires each model's HARD PREDICTIONS to build consensus pseudo-labels, learn its scalar ε, compute the best-model posterior, and select via EIG; CODA (arXiv 2507.23771, source-verified) requires each classifier's PREDICTED CATEGORY per point to build Dawid–Skene confusion matrices. A scalar/loss tensor does not encode predicted classes (many predictions map to the same loss; a multi-hot loss discards the confusion structure entirely). Therefore POS criterion 2 ('some label-adaptive pi with LCB[G]>0 in ALL cohorts'), NEG-B's 'for EVERY pi' spurious-gain test, and §3.4 check C ('MODEL SELECTOR/CODA P_Best -> KNOWN best; beats P0') CANNOT be evaluated for the very methods C87 exists to add. The gate would emit CONTROL_PASS having validated only P0 + the risk-estimator family (LURE/VMA/ASE), never exercising the label-adaptive selection path nor testing it for manufactured gain — defeating §6.6's mandate that controls run 'the SAME modules C87E uses (selector, ...)'.
blockers.fix: Change the synthetic generator (both §3.4 and §6.1) to emit, per (candidate a, record r), a full PREDICTION object (categorical/multi-hot predicted label distribution or hard label) together with a KNOWN true multi-hot label y(r); derive the loss tensor from (prediction, label) via the registered loss so the SAME predictions drive LURE/VMA/ASE (losses), MODEL SELECTOR (hard predictions + consensus pseudo-labels), and CODA (confusion matrices). Plant a controlled prediction/confusion structure so that in POS a label-adaptive method genuinely beats P0, and in NEG-B no method beats P0. Only then can CONTROL_PASS certify the label-adaptive path and its spurious-gain resistance.

blockers.where: P-22, SPEC 4.A (T_e, D_e, a*H_e, a#_e), E6/§4.6 transport gate, §4.5 bootstrap; controls §6.2 (SEP_POS well-separated best)
blockers.issue: Winner's-curse bias in the FULL-view ceiling and standardizer, biased TOWARD the pre-registered 'expected' nontransport conclusion. a*H_e = argmin over 648 held losses and a#_e = argmax are extremized in-sample order statistics: the min is optimistically low, the max pessimistically high. Because a*C_e is selected on the disjoint acquisition view (out-of-sample => unbiased held loss) while the min is optimistic, T_e = L^H(a*C_e) - L^H(a*H_e) is systematically INFLATED — i.e., the transport gap is overstated and the E6/tau_T classification is biased toward NON-TRANSPORT, exactly the C86D-reproducing result the study hypothesizes. D_e = max-min is inflated (deflating T~ and G~). The bootstrap re-selects argmin/argmax per replicate so it propagates selection VARIANCE, but not this BIAS; BCa's bias-correction targets bootstrap-distribution median-bias, not max/min optimism over 648 correlated (adjacent-epoch) checkpoints, and BCa's smoothness assumption is violated by the non-differentiable argmin/argmax, so nominal coverage is not guaranteed in the near-tie regime. The POS/NEG/CALIB controls plant a WELL-SEPARATED best (SEP_POS=0.05), so they never stress the dense near-tie regime where this bias/miscoverage bites — the controls cannot catch it.
blockers.fix: Cross-fit the held ceiling: select a*H_e (and a#_e) on one held sub-sample of patients and evaluate L^H on a disjoint held sub-sample, averaging over folds/repeats — or apply an explicit selection-bias correction (e.g. bootstrap optimism estimate) to T_e, R_e, D_e. Register this before freeze. Additionally add a CONTROL world with a DENSE set of near-tied candidates (no single well-separated best) and verify the corrected ceiling/transport estimator is unbiased and the patient-cluster BCa interval attains nominal coverage under argmin instability.

blockers.where: P-22/SPEC 4.A (D_e, s_e), P-25/SPEC 4.D (tau_G=0.10, eps_e=0.25*s_e), P-23 (G~,T~ standardized by D_e; E4 by s_e)
blockers.issue: Non-robust standardizer with an unjustified threshold, risking a structural NO-ADVANTAGE independent of the science. The primary gate is LCB_95(G~) >= tau_G=0.10 with G~ = G/D_e and D_e = best-to-WORST span over 648 checkpoints. Cadence range(4,200,5) means the worst-of-648 a#_e is almost surely a near-chance epoch-4 checkpoint, so D_e is dominated by the distance from the best model to a degenerate one and is set by a single outlier. Requiring the active method to recover 10% of the best-to-near-chance span over uniform may be unreachable by construction, pre-ordaining 'NO ACTIVE ADVANTAGE' regardless of any real effect. It is also internally inconsistent: G~/T~ are scaled by the span D_e while the near-optimal margin eps_e=0.25*s_e is scaled by the SD s_e — two different scale references for the same cohort — and tau_G is never justified in D_e units.
blockers.fix: Standardize G (and T) by a ROBUST spread of held losses — e.g. s_e = SD_a L^H, the interquartile range, or the best-vs-median gap — rather than the full max-min span dominated by a near-chance checkpoint; or pre-register D_e computed after trimming degenerate/near-chance checkpoints. Re-derive tau_G in the chosen unit with an explicit argument that it is achievable, and use ONE consistent scale reference for tau_G and eps_e. Decide by design, not by any outcome.

blockers.where: P-20 (single deterministic hash split) vs P-25/SPEC 4.D ('K=10 seeds x M=5 acquisition/held splits'; 'split is an added resample layer inside the cluster bootstrap'); §4.5
blockers.issue: Internal contradiction and under-specified variance object. P-20 pins ONE deterministic split (key=SHA-256('C87_TARGET_SPLIT_V1'|cohort|patient_id), lower floor(n/2)->ACQUISITION), and §5.6/§4.1 assert the held view is SEALED with no patient across views. But SPEC 4.D says M=5 acquisition/held splits and nests them inside the patient-cluster bootstrap. If M=5, (a) the single deterministic split rule is violated and 5 salts are not pinned; (b) a patient is in the held view in some splits and the acquisition view in others, so 'sealed held' and 'no patient across views' hold only within a split; (c) nesting a train/test split resample inside a patient bootstrap yields a variance object whose BCa nominal coverage for the split-averaged endpoint is not established and is never checked by CALIB (which validates a single-world coverage, not the M-split layer).
blockers.fix: Reconcile explicitly: either (i) M=1 — drop the '5 splits' and keep the single frozen P-20 split; or (ii) pin 5 split salts as a NO-OUTCOME rule, define the estimand as the split-averaged endpoint with the M-layer materialized INSIDE the cluster bootstrap, restate the sealing/no-cross-view property per-split, and add a CALIB coverage check for that exact split-averaged, patient-clustered object.

blockers.where: §4.5 (per-cohort bootstrap) vs §6.4 CALIB ('paired/common-random-number bootstrap across policies for G')
blockers.issue: The operative gate endpoint G = R_{e,P0,B} - R_{e,pi,B} is a paired difference, but §4.5 (the load-bearing main-gate bootstrap) never states that R_P0 and R_pi are evaluated on the SAME patient resample per replicate. Only CALIB mentions CRN/paired. An unpaired resample would give an invalid/inflated CI for LCB_95(G~) — the exact statistic the gate keys on.
blockers.fix: State in §4.5/SPEC 4.F that every policy's loss is evaluated on a COMMON (paired / common-random-number) patient resample within each bootstrap replicate, matching CALIB §6.4.

blockers.where: P-12, §3.1(d), §3.2/§3.3 table, §4.6 registry line, Appendix ('Matsuura & Hara 2024')
blockers.issue: Citation-fidelity error for VMA: the Springer 2024 paper (DOI 10.1007/s10994-024-06603-1) is authored 'Hara, S., Matsuura, M., Honda, J. et al.' — Satoshi Hara is FIRST author and Honda et al. are dropped. The doc renders it 'Matsuura & Hara', reversing lead authorship. (Separately, the method is verified as an ADAPTIVE labeling strategy estimating pairwise loss-difference signs — so its query distribution IS updated from queried labels; the doc's 'prediction-driven + adaptive query dist' tier is defensible but is stricter than the PI's literal 'updates model preference from queried labels' definition. Not a blocker since MODEL SELECTOR cleanly satisfies the label-adaptive requirement.)
blockers.fix: Cite as 'Hara, Matsuura, Honda et al., Machine Learning 2024' throughout; keep the finer taxonomy but add one clause noting VMA is label-adaptive at the sampling-distribution level (updates the pairwise preference from labels) though it maintains no best-model posterior.

blockers.where: §3.1(c), §3.6, Appendix ('XWED could not be independently verified as a named estimator label')
blockers.issue: The honesty-flag is factually wrong and mislabels the object: XWED IS verifiable as 'Expected Weighted Disagreement,' the named ACQUISITION FUNCTION (not an estimator) in Kossen, Farquhar, Gal, Rainforth, 'Active Surrogate Estimators,' NeurIPS 2022 (arXiv 2202.06881). The estimator is the ASE; XWED is its acquisition target.
blockers.fix: Resolve the flag: state XWED = Expected Weighted Disagreement (ASE's acquisition function, Kossen 2022, verified); ASE = the surrogate estimator. Remove the 'unverified estimator label' caveat.

blockers.issue: The MODEL SELECTOR venue 'AISTATS 2025, PMLR v258' is not confirmed by the source (arXiv 2410.13609 abstract lists no venue). Author list is correct. CODA's 'ICCV 2025 Highlight' IS verified; Active Testing ICML 2021, ASE NeurIPS 2022, and VMA Springer-ML 2024 are verified.
blockers.fix: Confirm the MODEL SELECTOR publication venue against the PMLR/AISTATS proceedings before freeze, or cite it as 'arXiv 2410.13609' pending confirmation.

blockers.where: §3.0/§3.1(b), §3.2 (Active Testing classified 'prediction-driven')
blockers.issue: Active Testing/LURE's status as the clean 'prediction-driven (non-label-adaptive)' contrast depends on which surrogate variant is fixed: a SELF-SURROGATE that retrains on acquired test labels makes the proposal q label-adaptive-in-proposal (like VMA), confounding the prediction-driven-vs-label-adaptive axis the whole design rests on.
blockers.fix: Pin, in the §3.4 audit, the FIXED-surrogate (non-retraining) Active Testing variant for the ECG registry so LURE remains a genuinely prediction-driven contrast to MODEL SELECTOR; register the surrogate and its (non-)update rule explicitly.

redteam.notes:
Red-team scope: method & statistical fidelity. VERDICT: NEEDS_REVISION — 1 BLOCKER + 3 MAJOR survive.

VERIFIED FAITHFUL (do not change): LURE weight v_m and R_LURE (Farquhar et al. ICLR 2021 / Kossen et al. ICML 2021), E[v_m]=1, uniform-q identity; MODEL SELECTOR mechanics (best-model posterior, single-ε, hard predictions, multiplicative Bayes update, EIG acquisition) — source-confirmed; CODA (Dawid–Skene confusion, EIG = H(P_Best) − Σ_c π̂(c|x)H(P_Best^c) structure) — source-confirmed; the prediction-driven vs label-adaptive PRIMARY split (LURE prediction-driven + MODEL SELECTOR label-adaptive) satisfies the PI requirement. G = R_P0 − R_π correctly CANCELS the in-sample a*H_e term, so the primary active-gain gate is conservative w.r.t. the winner's-curse bias (that bias instead threatens the E6 transport DIAGNOSTIC — MAJOR-2).

HEADLINE BLOCKER: both synthetic gates (§3.4 registry-freeze audit and §6 CONTROL_PASS) build their worlds from a per-record LOSS tensor, but MODEL SELECTOR needs hard predictions/consensus pseudo-labels and CODA needs predicted-category confusion matrices — so neither gate can run the two label-adaptive PRIMARY methods it claims to validate, and NEG-B can never test them for manufactured gain. Fix = generate predictions+true-labels and derive losses from them.

The three MAJORs (ceiling winner's-curse bias toward the hypothesized nontransport + non-robust max−min D_e standardizer with unjustified/possibly-unreachable τ_G=0.10 + M=5-splits contradicting the single deterministic P-20 split and unvalidated nested-bootstrap coverage) are all fixable by design-only edits (cross-fit ceiling, robust spread + re-justified threshold, reconcile split count and add coverage check) with no data or model read. All flagged items are resolvable without reading any target-cohort model outcome, consistent with the freeze constraints.

Sources: Hara/Matsuura/Honda 2024 (link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10994-024-06603-1); MODEL SELECTOR (arxiv.org/abs/2410.13609); CODA (arxiv.org/abs/2507.23771, github.com/justinkay/coda); ASE/XWED (arxiv.org/abs/2202.06881, neurips.cc/virtual/2022/poster/54273).

conflicts: [RC-1] Method-registry classification: §4 SPEC 4.F listed the C86D-lineage names A1/A2H as the prediction-driven method and grouped Active-Testing/LURE with the label-adaptive methods; §3 (authoritative) classifies Active-Testing/LURE as PREDICTION-DRIVEN and MODEL SELECTOR/CODA as the GENUINELY LABEL-ADAPTIVE methods. Reconciled: SPEC 4.F rewritten to reference §3's registry (P0 + LURE + MODEL SELECTOR mandatory, CODA conditional); A1/A2H dropped from the ECG registry. Gate machinery is method-agnostic, so no gate logic changed.

[RC-2] Budget ladder: §4 PINS B in {0,8,16,32,64,128,256,512} from cost realism; §3 framed the budget grid as DEFERRED to a metadata audit; §6 referred to it as 'the C87E interface-section budget grid'. Reconciled: §4's ladder is the single authoritative object; §3's deferral is subsumed (metadata cross-check satisfied: 512 <= ~5% of the ~10k targets, <=1.5% of Ningbo); §6's label corrected to the §4 endpoints/estimand ladder, reused verbatim.

[RC-3] Selection pool A_e: §5 pins A_e = FULL 648-model zoo (pooled across 8 contexts); §3 and §6 repeatedly describe an '81-candidate zoo'. Reconciled to 648 pooled (§5 owns the zoo); '81' is the per-context building block; §4 confirms endpoint/gate algebra is invariant to per-context-81 vs pooled-648.

[RC-4] Ningbo patient-unit: §1/§2 assert one-ECG-per-patient (rule G-b, record=patient); §4/§5 flagged Ningbo as a multiple-records-per-patient case (rule G-c, the 'live G-c case'). Web-verified against PhysioNet ecg-arrhythmia v1.0.0 that BOTH Chapman-Shaoxing and Ningbo are one 10-s ECG per patient. Reconciled to G-b for both; residual caveat (Challenge distribution ships no patient-ID field, so G-b rests on the source descriptor read by the SPEC 4.E audit); operationally identical (record-level clustering either way).

[RC-5] PTB-XL counts: Challenge-2021 distribution lists 21,837 records; native PTB-XL v1.0.3 = 21,799 records / 18,869 patients (18,885 is the canonical Wagner-2020 patient count used in §1/§2/§5). Reconciled: Challenge-file reads use 21,837; native patient-linkage reads use 21,799/18,869. PTB-XL is source-side and enters no target endpoint.

[RC-6] Scored-class count: §4 cites |S| <= 26; §3/§6 cite 30 scored. Reconciled: Challenge-2021 scored 30 nominal diagnoses with equivalent-class merges yielding ~26 distinct scored classes; exact |S| fixed by Rule L / dx_mapping_scored.csv. Only material if the selection loss uses multi-label S (see R-1).

[RC-7] T3 (rate) source-feasibility cross-dependency: §2 flagged that SB/STach have 0/45 support in CPSC panels, so T3 is trainable only in PTB-XL-inclusive source panels, and could be dropped 'if any panel is CPSC-only'. Resolved: §5 source domains D0 = {PTB-XL, CPSC2018, CPSC-Extra} are present in EVERY panel, so PTB-XL is always available and T3 is not dropped for panel reasons. Residual sub-flag R-2 routed to the metadata audit.

[R-1] RESIDUAL (not cleanly reconciled): selection-loss label axis. §2 freezes a SINGLE BINARY TASK as 'the task used in C87E'; §4 defines the primary selection loss as mean binary NLL over the multi-label scored set S (|S| <= 26/30); §5 trains a multi-label head. The NLL functional form is common; the label axis of the selection loss is not. Changing §4's primary loss requires a section-owner decision. Flagged, with NO-OUTCOME ratification rule D-24 (pin (a) single §2 task or (b) multi-label S before C87E); editor recommendation = (a).

[R-2] RESIDUAL sub-flag: §5's support-level-1 OACI missing-cell deletion (D-17) must be constrained so it never deletes the finally-selected task's positive class from its sole supporting source cohort (e.g., SB from PTB-XL), which would silently break T3 trainability in the 4 support-level-1 contexts. Routed to the metadata audit; flag preserved.

Minor bookkeeping (reconciled, recorded): PTB-XL patient count 18,885 vs 18,869; scored-class count 26 vs 30; 'XWED' estimator label could not be independently verified (ASE = Active Surrogate Estimators, Kossen 2022, is verified). None affects a target endpoint.
discrepancies: No PI cohort figure is contradicted: CPSC 6,877 / CPSC-Extra 3,453 / PTB-XL 21,837 / Georgia 10,344 / Chapman-Shaoxing 10,247 / Ningbo 34,905 all match the canonical Challenge-2021 training-split counts exactly.

PI figures are challenge TRAINING-SUBSET record counts, not raw source-database sizes: raw Chapman-Shaoxing (Zheng 2020) = 10,646 patients; raw Georgia/Emory ~20,672 across train/val/test; raw Ningbo (ecg-arrhythmia v1.0.0 source) = 40,258 (Challenge subset 34,905). PTB-XL 21,837 ~= raw; CPSC 6,877 = raw CPSC2018 training.

Independence caveat (must correct any 'three independent cohorts' wording): Chapman-Shaoxing 10,247 + Ningbo 34,905 = 45,152 = the single combined Zheng ecg-arrhythmia v1.0.0 database (web-verified); the two targets share one SNOMED-CT labeling pipeline and one physician-adjudication protocol, both in Zhejiang Province -> only TWO independent lineages among the three targets (Emory/USA + shared Zheng/Chapman lineage).

AF (164889003) has 0 records in Ningbo (which carries 7,615 atrial-flutter 164890007) -> candidate T1 AF-vs-NSR is EXPECTED INADMISSIBLE for the 3-cohort gate; the frozen rule refuses to union AFL into AF to rescue support (surfaced, not overridden).
RATE slot resolves to Sinus Bradycardia 426177001 (target support GEO 1677 / CHA 3889 / NIN 12670) as primary, Sinus Tachycardia 427084000 fallback; Sinus Arrhythmia 427393009 excluded (Chapman = 0).

BBB slot resolves to RBBB-union {59118001, 713427006} (Challenge-declared CRBBB=RBBB equivalent; union GEO 570 / CHA 454 / NIN 1291 clears the floor) rather than LBBB 164909002 (Ningbo 35 < floor); incomplete RBBB 713426002 excluded as a distinct concept.
T3 (rate) is trainable only in PTB-XL-inclusive source panels (SB/STach 0/45 in CPSC panels); resolved because every §5 source panel contains all three source domains incl PTB-XL.

PTB-XL count: Challenge-2021 v1.0.3 distribution lists 21,837 records; native PTB-XL v1.0.3 = 21,799 records / 18,869 patients. Use 21,837 for Challenge files, 21,799/18,869 for native patient-linkage. Patient count cited as 18,885 (canonical Wagner-2020) in §1/§2/§5 vs 18,869 (native v1.0.3) in §4 — minor source-side revision difference.

Modality/labels caveat: Challenge-2021 is MULTI-LABEL SNOMED-CT (133 codes encoded; 30 nominal scored, ~26 after equivalence-merge), whereas MODEL SELECTOR (single scalar error eps) and CODA (single-label confusion matrices) are described for single-label multiclass -> require a registered multi-label reduction (§3 audit check E / D-12).

Ningbo records-per-patient: §1/§2 (one-ECG-per-patient, G-b) vs §4/§5 (multiple records/patient, G-c). Web-verified source = one-ECG-per-patient for both Chapman-Shaoxing and Ningbo; reconciled to G-b. The Challenge distribution ships no patient-ID field, so G-b rests on the source descriptor (SPEC 4.E audit adopts it).

'XWED' could not be verified as a named estimator in the literature; ASE = Active Surrogate Estimators (Kossen et al. 2022, arXiv:2202.06881) is verified. Recommend the method section confirm or drop the 'XWED' label; Section 6 is estimator-agnostic so it is unaffected.

Patient-level split is literally executable only for one-ECG-per-patient cohorts; Georgia patient linkage is undocumented (no ID field). Resolved by the NO-OUTCOME SPEC 4.E patient-grouping audit (rule, not value); no PI intent weakened.

Forced modality adaptation: the EEG engine's single-label softmax-CE task term becomes per-class BCE for multi-label SNOMED, and the OACI (domain,class) cell 'class' axis is redefined over SNOMED codes; the OACI/SRC wrappers stay byte-identical (flagged, not silently frozen).
Selection pool granularity: §5 registers the full 648-model pooled zoo; §3/§6 describe 81 per context. Reconciled to 648 pooled; §4 gate algebra invariant.

SPEC 4.F registry text mis-classified methods (A1/A2H as prediction-driven; Active-Testing/LURE as label-adaptive); §3 authoritative reclassification adopted (LURE = prediction-driven; MODEL SELECTOR/CODA = label-adaptive).

PTB vs PTB-XL bundling: Challenge-2021 reports a combined PTB+PTB-XL group (~22,353 ECGs); the design uses PTB-XL only (21,837) and EXCLUDES the small PTB set (516), so the source PTB-XL count is 21,837, not 22,353.
open_no_outcome: Rule R / C87-RATE (D-01): unified sampling rate r* in {100,250,500} Hz by downsample-only integer decimation (500 mod r == 0) + signal-integrity audit (spectral-energy retention, QRS morphology fidelity, diagnostic-band preservation up to f_diag) + resource envelope; smallest admissible r, ties -> larger bandwidth; content-hashed before training. Audit reads only waveforms + resource meters.
Rule W (D-02): fix a single window = 10 s = 10*r* samples (5,000 at 500 Hz), crop / right-zero-pad uniformly; length pinned from the duration-metadata audit.
Rule Q / QC (D-03): flat-lead count K and minimum native-duration floor finalized in the signal-integrity audit (header/waveform statistics only); reason-code C87-E; no performance-based exclusion.

Patient-unit per cohort (D-04, Rule G / OD-2 / SPEC 4.E G-a/G-b/G-c): documented linkage -> patient; documented one-record-per-patient -> record=patient (Chapman + Ningbo, per source doc); no linkage -> metadata near-duplicate audit, default record-proxy-with-caveat, else report-but-exclude. Georgia = record=patient unless an ID field is found. Reads zero model outputs.

Rule L / scored-label set S (D-05): intersection of Challenge-2021 scored SNOMED classes present in ALL included cohorts (dx_mapping_scored.csv), gated by per-class positive-support counts only; |S| via equivalence-merge.
OD-1 (D-06): the §2.7 algorithm executed on counts recomputed from downloaded v1.0.3 .hea headers is the single binding task, committed as a signed manifest BEFORE any candidate is scored.
OD-3/OD-4 (D-07): T2 = RBBB-union {59118001,713427006} unless only LBBB {164909002} passes the gate; T3 = SB {426177001} unless maximin support selects STach {427084000}. Decided by support counts.

OD-5 / source-feasibility (D-08): per-panel class-presence (>= tau_src) evaluated vs the §5 panel compositions; RESOLVED (all panels include all 3 source domains incl PTB-XL); residual R-2 (support-level-1 deletion must not remove the selected positive class from its sole source) routed to the metadata audit.
OD-6 / empty-admissible (D-09): if no candidate passes the gate on downloaded metadata, return DEV_STOP; no threshold relaxation, no distinct-concept union.
OD-7 / Georgia length (D-10): 5-10 s vs 10 s handled by the interface window rule (right-zero-pad); does not enter task selection.

Primary label-adaptive membership (D-11): {MODEL SELECTOR, CODA} PRIMARY iff they pass the §3.4 synthetic toy-oracle reference-fidelity audit (A-E) AND admit a registered multi-label + patient-cluster mapping; else SECONDARY. Synthetic-toy behavior + public-code numerical agreement only.

Decomposable pointwise loss (D-12): fixed decomposable per-record acquisition loss so LURE/VMA/ASE unbiasedness holds; if the deployment metric is non-decomposable (macro-F1 / Challenge weighted score) register the additive acquisition surrogate separately from the held-view T/R/G metric.
Reference-implementation pinning (D-13): content-address + pin jlko/active-testing, okanovic/model-selector, justinkay/coda BEFORE the audit (signal-integrity check against exact versions).

Multi-candidate query rules (D-14): VMA O(K)/O(K^2) multi-model rule + Active-Testing shared-proposal-plus-per-candidate-LURE selection rule pinned against reference code / re-derivation in the audit before freeze.

Patient-cluster sampling adaptation (D-15): one query = one RECORD while inference is PATIENT-clustered; each method's without-replacement weighting shown cluster-valid on the toy or the exact cluster-level modification registered (audit check D).
1D backbone architecture (D-16): depth/width/kernel/pooling of the shared 1D residual CNN pinned by a reference-implementation rule, spec-hash frozen before training, never tuned on any cohort.

Support-level-1 deletion cell (D-17): the single (source-cohort, SNOMED-class) cell deleted from source-train = a mid-prevalence diagnostic class present in >=2 source cohorts, deleted from exactly one; resolved in the metadata audit; target/held views byte-invariant.
Min-support gate (D-18): N_min patients/view and m_class records/scored-class/view fixed by the metadata audit; a cohort/class failing -> C87-E, no re-split, no threshold change.
Source panel audit fraction + salts (D-19): fixed pre-registered constants (C87_SOURCE_PANEL_A/B_V1) set before training (partition-sensitivity axis), not outcome-driven.
Cohort inclusion in the formal gate (D-20): INCLUDED = untouched cohorts passing the SPEC 4.E audit, decided metadata-only before any outcome so exclusion cannot bias the gate.
Standardizer/ceiling numeric values (D-21): D_e, s_e, T_e formulas frozen (SPEC 4.A); values computed at analysis from candidate held losses that do not yet exist.
Degenerate-span guard (D-22): a cohort whose candidate held-loss span D_e sits inside patient-cluster bootstrap noise is flagged 'no selection signal' (neither pass nor fail).

Control structural params (D-23): A_syn (=648 pooled zoo / 81 per context), E=3, B_grid ({0,8,16,32,64,128,256,512}), N_p^(e), records-per-patient cluster-size, per-record additive loss, and policy set taken verbatim from the frozen C87E config / public metadata; effect/correlation constants (SEP_POS=0.05, TAU_NEG=0.05, phi=0.15, rho, kappa, K>=1000, alpha=0.05, coverage band [93.5%,96.5%]) frozen a priori.

Selection-loss label axis (D-24, RECONCILIATION RULE for residual R-1): before C87E a single owner pins whether the primary selection loss is (a) binary NLL on the single §2-selected task or (b) mean binary NLL over the multi-label scored set S; editor-recommended = (a). Decided by design, not by any outcome.
